description_long: This bungalow features a single-story structure with a prominent cross-gabled roof covered in gray shingles. The front facade includes a central entrance with a small porch and two supporting columns, flanked by symmetrical windows. The exterior walls are finished in a light color, likely stucco or painted siding, and the house sits on a rectangular green lawn. On one side of the property, there are three large signboards or panels, possibly for decoration or information. The rear of the bungalow has a small set of steps leading to a secondary entrance, and the windows are arranged to provide balanced natural light. The overall design is compact and functional, typical of suburban residential neighborhoods.
description: A single-story bungalow with a cross-gabled gray shingle roof, light exterior walls, a central porch, and symmetrical windows, set on a green lawn.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with a porch, two columns, and three signboards on the left side.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the gabled roof, side windows, and the signboards extending along the property.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a small set of steps to a secondary entrance and a simpler facade with fewer windows.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the cross-gabled roof, large rear windows, and the paved walkway leading to the front porch.
